dialogue.utterance: Unconscious patient at Apt #205, reported by a staff member.
Hi, I'm [NAME], with Rescue Squad 96. Can you tell me what brought you to call 911 today?
Chief complaint is unconsciousness - patient was found on the floor in the kitchenette.
Patient is unresponsive with a GCS of 3 - eye opening, verbal, and motor are all 1.
Airway is obstructed and requires jaw-thrust to open - no audible obstructions present.
Agonal respirations at 10 per minute - assisted ventilation is required.
Radial pulse is strong, rapid, and regular - skin is warm and slightly cyanotic.
Pupils are fixed and pinpoint - pupils unreactive.
Jaw thrust performed and airway secured - BVM initiated for assisted breathing.
Blood glucose was measured by fire department prior to our arrival - no value shared.
Oxygen saturation is 75% on room air - indicates poor oxygenation.
Skin is clammy, mild cyanosis observed - no bites, rashes, or wounds noted.
18-gauge IV line started in left forearm and flushed with 10 mL saline.
Administered 0.5 mg Narcan IV - patient exhibits response within 1-2 minutes.
Non-rebreather placed at 10 LPM - pulse ox rising from 75% to near normal.
Limb lift with 4 responders - patient placed on cot and stabilized for transport.
No medical emergency lights used - transport is to [COMPANY_NAME] ER.
Destination is [COMPANY_NAME] for definitive care - no complications during move.
Calling the ER now - confirmed heroin use by friend on scene.
Fires obtained basic vitals - patient is now alert following Narcan administration.
Field impression was opioid overdose - improved after Narcan.
Patient is alert and self-ambulatory at time of transport.
ODEMSA tag exchanged - friend reported heroin history before onset.
No witnessed event - patient found by staff in kitchenette.
No trauma noted - only extensive calcification on lower extremities observed.
No change in pupils post Narcan - still pinpoint and fixed.
Patient is transported in a position of comfort - no medical emergency lights used.
Airway remains patent with jaw-thrust - BVM is being maintained.
No flaring nostrils or chest rise asymmetries - shallow assisted breathing.
Normal thoracic structure - assisted breaths are shallow.
Pulse is 165 bpm - strong and regular as per fire department initial assessment.
Oxygen saturation is 94% - after 10 minutes on non-rebreather.
Respiratory rate is 20 per minute - shallow with BVM assistance.
Fire department reports 189/84 - no change since initial field assessment.
No stridor or neck masses observed.
12-lead EKG shows sinus tachycardia - no acute changes observed.
No deformities or injuries to extremities - sensation intact post Narcan.
EKG shows frequent supraventricular complexes and left anterior fascicular block.
Patient secured on cot - no physical restraints needed.
Handoff to [COMPANY_NAME] nurse complete - everything noted on EMS tag.
Patient is alert and self-ambulatory at time of transport.
Patient lost consciousness - was on the floor in kitchenette.
Unable to obtain current medication history - patient sedated initially.
No signs of back pain or injury - patient seated with assistance.
Fully alert and oriented after Narcan administration.
Room Green 3 is assigned - ER staff on alert.
Field impression was opioid overdose - improved after Narcan.
No visible trauma - patient is edematous in lower extremities.
No delays in scene clearance - ambulance accessible to patient.
Patient re-clothed in soft-shell jacket - no impeding equipment.
Ambulance transported with full supplies - ready for changes.
Traffic plan was static - no expeditious transport needed.
ER was prenotified via phone - all forms completed and signed.
AIC signed tag both as AIC and crew - no incident reported.
No post-handoff actions needed - patient condition stable.
Ambulance returned empty - driver for return trip available immediately.
No follow-up reports - patient assigned to Room Green 3 on transfer.
All care and documentation completed - provider sign-out complete.
No return on scene - care transferred to [COMPANY_NAME].
Patient is terminal transfer - all four staffing maintained until post-handoff.
Patient has a history of primary hypertension - no allergies or meds reported.
Patient was near-fainting as reported - response to Narcan indicated recovery.
